_location_comment: Home location, shared by the geo-aware monitors (quakes, weather). Update these to move every location-based alert at once.

quiet_hours._comment: Overnight do-not-disturb. When enabled, pushes at low/default/min priority are dropped silently between start and end (local time = UTC + utc_offset_hours; DR is -4, no DST). High/urgent alerts (earthquakes, hurricanes, tsunami advisories) always ring through, and the manual test push is never suppressed. Disabled by default; set enabled=true to turn it on. Times are 'HH:MM' 24h; the window may wrap midnight.

priority._comment: Personal Priority Engine. Every topic emits a normalized event; the first matching rule sets a cross-topic score (default if none), boosts add, overrides clamp. score>=threshold pushes now (ntfy priority from ntfy_bands: >=90 urgent, >=70 high, else default - and quiet_hours holds default/low overnight while high/urgent ring through); digest_floor<=score<threshold buffers to the daily digest; below digest_floor is dropped. Rules match by topic and optional severity (info<low<moderate<high<critical). Remove this whole section to turn the engine OFF (every topic reverts to its legacy push/digest).

quakes._comment: Nearby earthquakes (USGS). Routed by magnitude AND great-circle distance from location: live push within live_radius_km at >= live_min_mag, daily digest within digest_radius_km at >= digest_min_mag, else dropped. A large, shallow quake within tsunami_radius_km (>= tsunami_min_mag, depth <= tsunami_max_depth_km) instead fires an urgent 'check tsunami advisories' alert.

weather._comment: Tropical storm / hurricane alerts (US National Hurricane Center Atlantic ATOM feed). An entry naming a region_term goes live if it also carries a live_term (watch/warning), else to the daily digest. Off-region Atlantic activity is ignored, so it stays quiet outside threats to our area.

air_quality._comment: Local air quality (Open-Meteo, US AQI). Alerts when AQI enters a band at or above alert_band_index (0=Good,1=Moderate,2=Unhealthy for sensitive,3=Unhealthy,4=Very unhealthy,5=Hazardous), at most once per worsening band per day.

fx._comment: USD -> DOP exchange rate (open.er-api, free). Pushes when the rate crosses out of the [low, high] band (low/high or back inside). Tune low/high to the range you care about.

twitch._comment: Streamers to watch for going live (via decapi.me, no key). One push per live session; re-arms after they go offline.

music._comment: followed_artists -> new-release alerts (Deezer, no key). Daily discovery picks a song from a Deezer related-artist seeded by your library (data/music_seed.json) that you probably haven't heard.

blood_donation._comment: Reminds when you're eligible to donate whole blood again. Update last_donation each time you donate; renotify_days throttles repeat nudges while still eligible.

holidays._comment: Dominican Republic public holidays via Nager.Date (no key). Heads-up on each lead day before (and the day of, 0).

uv._comment: Daily max UV index for your location via Open-Meteo (no key). Pushes only when uv_index_max >= alert_uv. The DR runs ~8-9 in summer, so raise alert_uv if you only want extreme days.

marine._comment: Daily sea state for the nearest coast via Open-Meteo Marine (no key). Pushes only when wave_height_max >= rough_wave_m (a rough-seas heads-up for beach/fishing days). Coast coords default to Boca Chica.

launches._comment: Upcoming rocket launches via Launch Library 2 (no key). Alerts once per launch when it is within imminent_hours. skip_keywords drops routine launches (Starlink by default) to cut noise.

iss._comment: Visible-window ISS passes over your location via the g7vrd API (no key). Alerts a pass with max elevation >= min_elevation_deg that falls in an evening/pre-dawn viewing window.

anthropic._comment: Official Anthropic announcements (model releases, etc.) via Google News, filtered to posts whose source is Anthropic itself. No key.

fda._comment: New drug/biologic approvals (openFDA Drugs@FDA). Drugs@FDA carries NO indication/disease field, so these keywords are matched against the brand name + active ingredient(s) only, NOT the treated condition. Scoring is additive (a keyword hit boosts, it never gates), and a 'regulatory' source weight plus the 'approves' action term already clears the live tier, so EVERY original NDA/BLA approval alerts regardless of disease area; the keywords serve to surface agent matches (e.g. 'vaccine', 'influenza', 'hepatitis', 'gene therapy' can match an active ingredient) rather than to filter. application_types limits to NDA new drugs and BLA biologics (excludes high-volume ANDA generics).

_digest_comment: max_per_source caps how many items any one source (a film, game, or feed) may hold in the shared buffer, so a high-volume source can't evict the low-volume domain monitors before the daily flush. Evictions and the flush both rank by score, so the most important pending items survive and surface first.

games_scoring._comment: Importance scorer for game news headlines. score = source_weight + one-shot signal bonuses - one-shot noise penalties; tier = highest threshold met. Matching is case-insensitive SUBSTRING, so terms must not be substrings of innocent words (e.g. 'review' would match 'preview'). high -> live push, moderate -> daily digest, below moderate -> dropped.

movies_scoring._comment: Importance scorer for movie news headlines. score = source_weight + one-shot signal bonuses - one-shot noise penalties; tier = highest threshold met. Matching is case-insensitive SUBSTRING, so terms must not be substrings of innocent words (e.g. 'review' would match 'preview'). high -> live push, moderate -> daily digest, below moderate -> dropped.
